_about: Fallback pre-generated bio variants for first paint (and for when the live API fails). The live API (/bio) is what runs once a user interacts with any dial. Fallback set covers lens × length only (4 entries); model and vibe dimensions are only meaningful when the API generates.

builder.short: Senior design leadership at Bibel TV, ChurchDesk, Ninox, and Datameer. Shaping practice, hiring, design systems, and the harder calls — that's the shape of the job for me.
builder.long: Senior design leadership across four B2B SaaS companies over the past decade. At Bibel TV I came in as the first design hire and built the practice from scratch. At Ninox, ChurchDesk, and Datameer I led existing teams — design system governance, hiring, and making the harder calls when someone wasn't the right fit. The throughline isn't 'always first hire' — it's shaping the function from senior leadership wherever the function is at.

ai-native.short: I design AI products and use AI to design. Right now: vibe-coding for databases, Copilot skills for sensitive support, agentic prototyping inside engineering. Hands-on, not from the sidelines.
ai-native.long: I design AI products and I use AI to design. At Ninox I shaped the vibe-coding onboarding directly with the founders — prompt-to-database with the schema rendering into the real editor, not a chat preview. At Bibel TV the same year I designed a Copilot skill for customer support (handbook as context, human keeps the send decision) and run agentic prototyping with engineering — Figma to Claude Code to working prototype the same week. The throughline: AI works best when its output lands on a surface the user can actually touch, not in a chat transcript about the artefact.
